There are no tables in the source document, so this check is not applicable.

checks.a_analysis: The text content matches the source document exactly. Minor changes in formatting (e.g., bullets represented as dashes or flattened into a single paragraph string with newlines) are within the acceptable range for accessibility-optimized conversion and do not alter the meaning.

checks.a_analysis: The output does not contain any duplicated text or sections. Each slide's content is represented exactly once in the JSON structure.
The document uses H1 for slide titles and H2 for secondary headings on the slides. The hierarchy is consistent and does not skip levels (e.g., H1 to H2 to paragraph).
There are no tables in the source document.

On Page 2 (Slide 2), the reading order is logically disrupted. The footer text 'Geoportal Website: data.nconemap.com', which appears at the bottom of the source slide, is inserted into the output (reading_order 5) before the main list content 'Natural Heritage Program' (reading_order 6 and 7). This breaks the flow of information.
checks.b_evidence_source: The text 'Geoportal Website: data.nconemap.com' appears at the bottom of the slide, visually and logically after the 'Natural Heritage Program' list.
checks.b_evidence_output: The element with reading_order 5 ('Geoportal Website: data.nconemap.com') appears before the elements with reading_order 6 and 7 which contain the main slide content.

checks.a_analysis: Lists are not properly structured as list elements in the JSON. On Slide 2, the list items are merged into a single paragraph using manual hyphenation. On Slide 3, a complex multi-level list is flattened into a single paragraph (element index 9), losing all structural nesting and primary bullet markers for the top-level items ('Ongoing', 'Upcoming').
checks.b_evidence_source: A multi-level bulleted list on Slide 3 with items such as 'Ongoing', 'Infrastructure buildout', and 'Upcoming'.

checks.a_analysis: The URL 'data.nconemap.com' is present as plain text. While the pipeline did not create a clickable link object, the text is accurately captured from the source, and no fabricated links were added.

checks.a_analysis: The hierarchical lists from the source PDF (pages 2 and 3) have been flattened into single paragraphs in the HTML output. On page 3, the structure of the 'Tasks' section is significantly compromised as sub-bullets under 'Ongoing' and 'Upcoming' are merged into a single text block. The lack of proper delimiters or spacing between original list items (e.g., 'DB servers EPA parcels project implementation Upcoming') destroys the visual organization and makes the content difficult to parse, failing to maintain the visual flow and intent of the source document.
checks.b_evidence_source: Page 3 shows a hierarchical list with main bullets 'Ongoing' and 'Upcoming' and several indented sub-bullets under each.
checks.b_evidence_output: The 'Tasks' section is rendered as a single paragraph block: 'Ongoing Infrastructure buildout - Replicate data between web servers - Replicate all map/image services between data centers - Replicate geodatabases between DB servers EPA parcels project implementation Upcoming - Elevation download from Geoportal imagery tab - Website redesign/integration - NC OneMap - Geoportal'

checks.a_analysis: There are no tables in the source PDF.

The source PDF on page 3 uses three green checkmark icons as meaningful status indicators for specific tasks. The rendered HTML only shows one such icon at the very end of the section, meaning two of the three status icons are missing, and the association of the status with the specific tasks is lost.
checks.b_evidence_source: Page 3 contains three green checkmark icons next to specific sub-bullet items under 'Ongoing'.
checks.b_evidence_output: Only one green checkmark image is visible at the bottom of the rendered 'Tasks' section.

checks.a_analysis: Headings appear in the Table of Contents and in the body, which is expected accessible design. No body paragraphs or list items are duplicated in the output.
